title: Otis and the Missing Moon Rocks
synopsis: Young scientist Otis discovers that the moon rocks from his class's space museum field trip have mysteriously disappeared. With his friends Maya and Carlos, he uses scientific thinking to solve the case and finds an unexpected culprit.

pages.text: Otis loved science class. Today was extra special. His teacher, Ms. Chen, had brought real moon rocks to show everyone. "These came from space!" said Otis. His eyes grew big and round. The moon rocks sparkled in their special box. Maya and Carlos crowded around to see too. "Wow!" said Maya. "They look like glittery cookies!" Carlos giggled. "Don't eat them, Maya!"

illustration.description: A diverse classroom with young Otis (brown hair, curious expression) leaning over a display case containing sparkling moon rocks. Maya (Black girl with braids) and Carlos (Latino boy with glasses) stand nearby, all with wide, amazed eyes. Ms. Chen (Asian teacher) smiles in the background. Bright classroom colors with educational posters on the walls.
illustration.alt: Otis and his classmates examining moon rocks in their classroom

pages.text: Ms. Chen put the moon rocks away safely. "I'll lock them in my desk," she said. "Tomorrow we'll learn more about them." After lunch, Otis came back early. He wanted to peek at the moon rocks again. But when he looked at Ms. Chen's desk, something was wrong. The box was there, but it was empty! "Oh no!" Otis cried. "The moon rocks are gone!"

illustration.description: Otis standing shocked in front of Ms. Chen's open desk drawer, looking at an empty clear box. His mouth is open in surprise and his hands are on his cheeks. The classroom is dimly lit with afternoon sunlight streaming through windows, creating dramatic shadows that emphasize the mystery.
illustration.alt: Otis discovering the empty moon rock box

pages.text: Otis ran to find Maya and Carlos on the playground. "Help!" he called. "The moon rocks disappeared!" Maya dropped her jump rope. Carlos stopped swinging. "That's impossible," said Carlos. "Rocks can't just walk away!" "Maybe they flew back to the moon," joked Maya. But Otis was serious. "This is a mystery," he said. "And mysteries need science to solve them!"

illustration.description: Otis running urgently toward Maya and Carlos on a colorful playground. Maya holds a jump rope and looks surprised, while Carlos sits on a swing looking puzzled. Other diverse children play in the background. The scene has bright, sunny playground colors with blue sky and green grass.
illustration.alt: Otis telling his friends about the missing moon rocks on the playground

pages.text: The three friends became detectives. "First, we observe," said Otis. He pulled out a magnifying glass from his backpack. Maya giggled. "You carry that everywhere!" They went back to the classroom. Otis looked very carefully at Ms. Chen's desk. "Look!" he said. "Tiny sparkly bits!" There were little glittery pieces on the floor.

illustration.description: Otis crouched down with a large magnifying glass, examining sparkly bits on the classroom floor. Maya and Carlos peer over his shoulders with interested expressions. The magnifying glass shows enlarged glittery specks. The scene has detective-like lighting with focused attention on the clues.
illustration.alt: Otis using his magnifying glass to examine clues on the floor

pages.text: "The sparkles make a trail!" said Carlos. He was right. The tiny pieces led from the desk to the door. Then they went down the hallway. "Follow the clues!" said Maya. The trail led past the library. It went past the art room. Where was it going? Otis felt like a real scientist now. "Evidence always tells a story," he said proudly.

illustration.description: The three children following a trail of sparkling bits down a school hallway. They're walking in single file with Otis leading, pointing ahead excitedly. The hallway has colorful bulletin boards and classroom doors, with the glittery trail clearly visible on the floor like breadcrumbs.
illustration.alt: The children following the sparkly trail through the school hallway

pages.text: The sparkly trail ended at the cafeteria. "Why would moon rocks come here?" wondered Maya. Inside, they saw Mrs. Rodriguez, the lunch lady. She was cleaning tables. "Hi kids," she said with a smile. "Looking for something?" Otis explained about the missing moon rocks. Mrs. Rodriguez looked surprised. "Moon rocks? In my cafeteria? That would be very strange!"

illustration.description: The children at the cafeteria entrance talking to Mrs. Rodriguez, a friendly Latina lunch lady wearing an apron. She holds a cleaning cloth and looks genuinely surprised. The cafeteria has typical lunch tables and serving areas in the background, with warm, welcoming colors.
illustration.alt: The children talking to Mrs. Rodriguez in the school cafeteria

pages.text: Mrs. Rodriguez helped them look around. "Wait," she said. "I did find something strange today." She showed them her broom. It had sparkly dust in it! "I swept this up from under table three," she said. Maya pointed to the table. "There's more sparkles there!" Sure enough, tiny glittery bits were everywhere around that table.

illustration.description: Mrs. Rodriguez holding a broom with obvious glittery residue, showing it to the three children who look fascinated. Table three in the background has visible sparkly bits around it. The lighting emphasizes the glittery evidence with almost magical sparkles catching the light.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Rodriguez showing the children her broom with glittery dust

pages.text: "Who sits at table three?" asked Otis. He was thinking like a detective now. Mrs. Rodriguez checked her chart. "Let's see... Table three is for Mrs. Park's kindergarten class." Carlos gasped. "My little sister Emma is in that class!" Maya looked confused. "But why would kindergarteners take moon rocks?" Otis had an idea. "Let's go ask them!"

illustration.description: The children and Mrs. Rodriguez looking at a seating chart on the cafeteria wall. Carlos looks worried about his sister while Otis points thoughtfully at the chart. Maya looks puzzled. The chart shows colorful table assignments with cute drawings next to names.
illustration.alt: Looking at the cafeteria seating chart to identify suspects

pages.text: They found Mrs. Park's class in the art room. The kindergarteners were painting pictures. "Hi Emma!" called Carlos to his little sister. Emma had paint on her nose and a big smile. "Carlos! Look at my painting!" She held up her paper. It showed the moon and stars. And something else that made Otis gasp. Her painting had real sparkly bits glued on it!

illustration.description: A busy art room with kindergarten children painting. Emma, a small Latina girl with paint-splattered clothes, proudly holds up a moon painting decorated with actual glittery moon rock pieces. Other children work on various art projects in the background, with art supplies and colorful paintings everywhere.
illustration.alt: Emma showing her moon painting decorated with real sparkly bits

pages.text: "Emma," said Carlos gently. "Where did you get those sparkly bits?" Emma looked proud. "I found them on our lunch table! They were so pretty, I saved them for my moon painting." Otis looked at Maya and Carlos. The mystery was solved! Emma had found the moon rock pieces that fell when someone moved the box. She didn't know they were special moon rocks.

illustration.description: Emma explaining innocently while gesturing at her artwork, surrounded by the older children who look relieved and understanding. Emma's expression is sweet and innocent, while Otis, Maya, and Carlos exchange knowing looks. The art room setting shows creative chaos with a warm, forgiving atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Emma innocently explaining how she found the sparkly bits for her art project

pages.text: "Emma didn't steal anything," Otis explained to Ms. Chen later. "She found pieces that fell out of the box." Ms. Chen smiled. "But where are the rest of the moon rocks?" They all looked in the box again. Then Carlos had an idea. "What if they fell through the crack in your desk drawer?" They looked underneath the desk. There they were! All the moon rocks, safe and sound.

illustration.description: Everyone gathered around Ms. Chen's desk as Carlos points underneath it. Ms. Chen kneels down reaching for the recovered moon rocks while the children watch excitedly. The scene shows relief and joy, with the moon rocks visible in the shadows under the desk, sparkling slightly.
illustration.alt: Finding the moon rocks under Ms. Chen's desk

pages.text: "Great detective work!" said Ms. Chen. "You used observation and evidence, just like real scientists." Emma got to keep her moon painting, but they made sure she knew the sparkles were very special. "Next time, ask before taking sparkly things," laughed Carlos. Otis felt proud. Science had helped them solve the mystery. "Every mystery has an answer," he said. "You just have to look carefully!"

illustration.description: A happy group celebration in the classroom with Ms. Chen praising the children. Otis stands proudly with his magnifying glass, Maya and Carlos beam with satisfaction, and little Emma holds her treasured moon painting. The moon rocks are safely back in their display box, and everyone looks joyful and accomplished.
illustration.alt: The happy resolution with everyone celebrating the solved mystery

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What did Otis use to look for clues?
questions.explanation: Otis used his magnifying glass that he carried in his backpack to examine the sparkly bits on the floor.

questions.question: Where did the sparkly trail lead the children?
questions.explanation: The trail of sparkly moon rock pieces led from Ms. Chen's classroom down the hallway to the cafeteria.

questions.question: Who found the sparkly bits first?
questions.explanation: Emma found the sparkly moon rock pieces on her lunch table and used them to decorate her moon painting.

questions.question: Where were most of the moon rocks hiding?
questions.explanation: Carlos figured out that the moon rocks had fallen through a crack in Ms. Chen's desk drawer and were underneath the desk.

questions.question: What did Otis say was needed to solve mysteries?
questions.explanation: Otis said that mysteries need science to solve them, and that they used observation and evidence like real scientists.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instruction: Help Otis solve the mystery by filling in the missing words!
pages.text: Otis loved __(1)__ class. When he found the moon rocks were missing, he decided to look for __(2)__. He used his __(3)__ to examine the sparkly bits on the floor. The trail led to the __(4)__ where Emma had been eating lunch.

pages.title: Detective Words
pages.instruction: Complete the sentences about being a detective like Otis!
pages.text: Good detectives __(1)__ carefully and look for __(2)__. They follow trails and ask __(3)__ to find answers. Otis learned that every mystery has a __(4)__ if you look hard enough!
